query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "fashionchameleon: towards real-time and interactive human-garment vide" computer science
rationale: The paper focuses on industrial motor fault diagnosis using LabVIEW and vibration analysis, which is an off-domain application of "real-time monitoring" unrelated to the user's question about extending a computer vision framework for human-garment video generation. This falls under the rejection rule for distinct constructs sharing only homonym keywords (e.g., "real-time" and "monitoring" appearing in both contexts but referring to entirely different mechanisms and domains).
